verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is an EGF-family ligand encoded as a single-pass type I transmembrane precursor (~170 aa) that is expressed on the cell surface before regulated shedding by ADAM10/ADAM17 releases the soluble ~46-aa EGF-like ectodomain. The TM-anchored proEREG form enables juxtacrine signaling and is documented on the cell surface (surface biotinylation, flow cytometry), but it is short-lived: sheddase cleavage is rapid and the soluble released form is the dominant biological actor engaging EGFR/ErbB4. This matches the 'transient TM precursor of a shed-ligand-dominant gene' pattern → contextual/dual_localization. No stable baseline surface pool; the canonical biology is overwhelmingly the shed soluble ligand.
